insights: Hardware tickets have a median resolution time of 420 hours—158.6% above the overall median (162.43 hours) and nearly 3× longer than Software tickets (141.82 hours), confirming Hardware as the single most severe bottleneck category.

Hardware tickets handled by the Hardware assignment group take a median of 429.60 hours to resolve, while the only Hardware ticket assigned to Network resolved in 146.40 hours—indicating assignment group capability or process alignment is a critical operational lever, not just category complexity.

Priority level drives the largest variation in Hardware resolution times (352.80-hour range), with 4-Low priority Hardware tickets taking 657.60 hours on average—over twice as long as 3-Moderate priority ones (304.80 hours)—revealing a counterintuitive inverse priority-efficiency relationship unique to Hardware.

Resolution times show a clear upward trend across the year: second-half median (167.98 hours) is 14.6% higher than first-half (146.64 hours), with months 10–12 averaging 271–308 hours—suggesting year-end operational pressure or resource depletion is amplifying Hardware’s existing delays.

Hardware exhibits extreme internal inconsistency: its interquartile range (399 hours) exceeds the full resolution-time span of all other categories combined, and its Q1 (283 hours) is already higher than the median resolution time of every non-Hardware category—indicating even the fastest Hardware resolutions are slower than typical performance elsewhere.

The 48 negative-resolution-time records (9.9% of dataset) signal systemic data integrity issues—particularly concerning timestamp reliability—which undermine confidence in temporal diagnostics and may distort observed trends if unaddressed in future reporting.
summary: The analysis confirms Hardware as the dominant driver of prolonged resolution times, with a median of 420 hours—158.6% above the overall median—and extreme variability that reflects deep-seated operational strain rather than isolated incidents. Priority and assignment group are the two strongest levers: low-priority Hardware tickets take longest to resolve, and Hardware-group handling adds ~3× the delay compared to cross-group resolution, pointing to capability gaps or procedural rigidity within the Hardware team. A clear year-end escalation in resolution times (months 10–12 averaging >270 hours) compounds this issue, suggesting seasonal capacity constraints are worsening an already critical bottleneck. Given the scale of delay, consistency of underperformance, and evidence of cross-functional divergence, immediate focus should be on root-cause investigation of Hardware-group workflows and priority-handling protocols—not broad process overhauls.
